steps.title: Use the zero product property
steps.content: The equation is already factored: $$x(x-13)(x+6)(x-5)^2=0.$$ A product equals $0$ when at least one factor equals $0$.

steps.title: List the solutions
steps.content: Set each factor equal to $0$: $$x=0, \quad x=13, \quad x=-6, \quad x=5.$$ The squared factor $(x-5)^2$ still gives the solution $x=5$.

steps.title: Choose the greatest solution
steps.content: Among $-6$, $0$, $5$, and $13$, the greatest solution is $13$.

steps.title: Confirm on the graph
steps.content: The graph crosses or touches the $x$-axis at $x=-6$, $0$, $5$, and $13$. The rightmost intercept is $13$, so the greatest solution is 13.
